summary: Promote the in-code dual-AC fixture to a frozen on-disk artifact with anti-tautology guard and verified CI wiring. Three atomic commits; test+fixture+CI only — production code untouched.

phases.label: Author frozen fixture

tasks.subject: Create fixture directory + graph.json
operations: Create directory src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/
Author graph.json with the 9-node graph (3 files × 3 nodes; 2 hs=missing + 1 hs=defined per file). Identical structural shape to seed test lines 122-167.
Node IDs follow seed convention: @Fn/{file}#{name}:{line}, @Cl/{file}#{name}:{line}, @C/{file}#{name}:{line}
Top-level shape: { version: '1.0', exportedAt: <ISO>, nodes: [...], edges: [], statistics: { nodeCount: 9, edgeCount: 0 } }

tasks.subject: Create source files + validation-report
operations: Author dual-ac-frozen/src/mod0.ts, mod1.ts, mod2.ts each with `export function fnN()`, `export class ClsN`, `export const CN` matching seed
Author dual-ac-frozen/validation-report.json: { header_missing_count: 3 } (file-grain canonical baseline)

tasks.subject: Document the invariant
operations: Author dual-ac-frozen/README.md describing: (1) the fixture's purpose, (2) the dual-AC identity relationship being asserted, (3) the 6/3 expected counts and how they're derived from fixture shape, (4) anchor note that 2415/263 are real-codebase numbers — NOT load-bearing for this fixture, (5) what to do if the rag-index graph schema changes (update fixture as part of that schema change, never silently)

tasks.subject: tsc gate after C1
operations: Run `tsc --noEmit` against tsconfig.json — must be clean
Run `tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.cli.json` — must be clean

phases.commit_message_template: feat(WO-RAG-INDEX-FROZEN-FIXTURE-DUAL-AC-INVARIANTS-001): author frozen fixture for dual-AC identity invariant
phases.phase_gate: C1 lands AND tsc clean both configs.

phases.label: Refactor AC-05 test to load frozen fixture + anti-tautology guard

tasks.subject: Replace procedural fixture-build with frozen-fixture load
operations: Locate the AC-05 dual-identity describe block (lines ~98-241 of current file)
Replace lines ~104-186 (the in-test fixture build) with code that copies src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/* into a tmpdir (the test's existing tempDir pattern)
Read graph.json and validation-report.json from the loaded fixture
Pass the fixture's projectDir to IndexingOrchestrator as today
Preserve the (a)/(b)/(c) Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT describe block (lines 32-70) untouched

tasks.subject: Anti-tautology guard
operations: Compute expected element-grain count by inspecting the loaded graph.json (count nodes with metadata.headerStatus === 'missing'). Do NOT use a hardcoded literal 6.
Compute expected file-grain count by inspecting the loaded graph.json (size of Set of files among hs=missing nodes). Do NOT use a hardcoded literal 3.
Read expected file-grain canonical from validation-report.json's header_missing_count and assert it equals the computed file-grain count from graph.json (cross-check inside the fixture)

Assertions: missingSkips.length === expectedElementGrain (AC-05a); uniqueFiles.size === expectedFileGrain (AC-05b); expectedElementGrain !== expectedFileGrain (distinctness; if the fixture ever changes to make these equal, the dual-AC framing collapses and the test must fail loudly)

tasks.subject: Test pass + tsc gate
operations: Run vitest against __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts — all describes PASS
Run vitest against src/integration/rag/__tests__/* — all PASS
Run `tsc --noEmit` both configs — clean

phases.commit_message_template: test(WO-RAG-INDEX-FROZEN-FIXTURE-DUAL-AC-INVARIANTS-001): load frozen fixture + anti-tautology guard for AC-05 dual identity
phases.phase_gate: C2 lands AND test green AND tsc clean both configs.

phases.label: Verify + lock CI wiring

tasks.subject: Verify default vitest run is blocking
operations: Run default `vitest run` (no scope flags) — confirm the AC-05 dual-identity test is included

Mutate the fixture: flip src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/graph.json so one hs=missing node becomes hs=defined. Confirm `vitest run` FAILS the AC-05 dual identity assertion. Revert the mutation before any commit.
Verify the failure exit code is non-zero (CI-blocking)

tasks.subject: Lock down wiring (if needed)
operations: If vitest config / package.json scripts already include the test in default run: document the verification in the commit message; no code change needed.
If anything is advisory-only or excluded by glob: lock it down via vitest config explicit include or package.json `test` script update.
If C3 ends up as a verification-only commit (no code change), still create the commit with a clear message — the verification itself is the closure artifact.

tasks.subject: Closeout-ready handoff
operations: Update communication.json status=complete with closeout-ready marker
Append CODEREF-CORE → ORCHESTRATOR communication_log entry summarizing the 3-commit chain + LOC delta + test results + mutation-guard demonstration
Hand off to ORCHESTRATOR for cross-project /close-workorder dispatch

acceptance_criteria.statement: /close-workorder produces ARCHIVED.md + tracking ledger updates + workorder_completed/closed events

decisions_record.decision: Frozen fixture lives under src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/dual-ac-frozen/ rather than at the existing seed test's location (__tests__/pipeline/)
decisions_record.rationale: The fixture is fundamentally a rag-integration artifact and belongs co-located with the rag tests for cohesion. The test that loads it can stay at __tests__/pipeline/ (continuing to share the same describe block as the Phase 7 chokepoint INVARIANT) — separation of fixture data from test logic is the standard pattern.

decisions_record.decision: Refactor the existing __tests__/pipeline/indexing-gate-invariant.test.ts AC-05 block in place rather than authoring a new test file at src/integration/rag/__tests__/dual-ac-frozen.test.ts
decisions_record.rationale: Refactor preserves git blame continuity, keeps the AC-05 assertions visually adjacent to the Phase 7 chokepoint invariants (which they semantically depend on), and avoids the orphan-test problem (two tests asserting the same property at different paths is a future maintenance trap). Trade-off: the test path doesn't strictly match dispatch-045's preferred boundary src/integration/rag/__tests__/* — but the FIXTURE does land in that boundary, which is what the dispatch's intent is really about.
decisions_record.boundary_clarification: Dispatch-045 boundary directive said 'src/integration/rag/__tests__/* + minimal CI wiring'. SKILLS interprets this as the FIXTURE landing in src/integration/rag/__tests__/fixtures/, while the test that loads it stays in its existing path. If ORCHESTRATOR wants the test moved as well, surface during execution review.

decisions_record.decision: Anti-tautology guard implementation: expected counts are computed from the loaded graph.json (the INPUT), not from a hardcoded literal and not from the IndexingResult (the OUTPUT)
decisions_record.rationale: If expected counts are hardcoded literals, a future fixture change can drift the input without triggering test re-validation. If expected counts are lifted from runtime output, the test is tautological (passes by definition). Computing from the loaded input fixture means: the test re-derives what the fixture should produce and asserts the production code produces that — independent paths from input to expected and input to actual.

decisions_record.decision: Distinctness check stays as `expect(elementGrain).not.toBe(fileGrain)` (assert different numbers) rather than asserting specific values like `not.toBe(6)` or `not.toBe(3)`
decisions_record.rationale: Identity-not-numbers principle from dispatch-045: the test asserts the RELATIONSHIP, not specific numerical values. If the fixture is later expanded to 5 files × 4 nodes, the distinctness check still works (10 element-grain ≠ 5 file-grain). Hardcoding numbers in the assertion would re-introduce the brittleness the WO is built to remove.

decisions_record.decision: C3 may be a verification-only commit (no code change) if CI wiring is already correct + blocking
decisions_record.rationale: The verification itself is the closure artifact. The mutation-guard demonstration in T3.1 is the load-bearing evidence; documenting the result in the commit message satisfies the WO's CI-wiring AC. Forcing a code change just to have a non-empty C3 would be ceremony without information yield.
